agent_output: {"domain_analysis":{"business_context":"A courier-tracking feature is being scoped where customers would see their courier's real-time location during delivery. Two internal stakeholder teams have issued directly contradictory, non-negotiable requirements: Customer Success demands full GPS transparency, while Operations demands no location sharing at all due to driver retention concerns.","bounded_context":"Delivery Tracking / Last-Mile Visibility","ubiquitous_language":[{"term":"Courier","definition":"The driver or delivery person assigned to transport a package from origin to customer destination."},{"term":"Real-time location","definition":"The GPS coordinates of a courier's device, updated at frequent intervals during an active delivery."},{"term":"Delivery window","definition":"The time period during which a courier is actively en route to deliver a specific order to a customer."},{"term":"Location sharing","definition":"The act of exposing some representation of a courier's position to an external party (the customer)."},{"term":"Driver retention","definition":"The operational metric tracking the ability to keep couriers on the platform; a key supply-side constraint in logistics."},{"term":"Last-mile visibility","definition":"The customer's ability to understand when and how their delivery will arrive during the final leg of transit."}]},"business_rules":[],"acceptance_criteria":[],"edge_cases":[{"scenario":"Courier is stationary for extended period (traffic, break, multi-drop)","expected_behavior":"Cannot define — depends on resolution of the core contradiction. Exact GPS would show the stop; abstracted ETA would mask it. Both have customer experience implications."},{"scenario":"Courier deviates from expected route","expected_behavior":"Cannot define — GPS transparency would expose this, raising customer concern. Hiding location removes accountability. Resolution depends on which stakeholder need prevails or what compromise is reached."},{"scenario":"Courier is near customer address but delivering to a neighbor first","expected_behavior":"Cannot define — exact coordinates could confuse/frustrate customer who sees courier 'right there' but not arriving. Abstracted view avoids this but reduces transparency."},{"scenario":"Courier ends shift or reassignment mid-delivery","expected_behavior":"Location tracking state must transition cleanly regardless of approach chosen. This is an invariant concern but specific behavior depends on the resolution."}],"business_impact":{"financial":"This is a two-sided marketplace problem. Showing exact GPS may improve customer conversion and reduce 'where is my order' support calls (estimated 30-40% of inbound CS volume in typical courier ops). However, losing couriers has direct financial cost: recruitment, onboarding, and reduced delivery capacity all erode margins. Neither side can be dismissed without quantified impact.","operational":"Driver churn directly constrains delivery capacity and SLA adherence. If Operations is correct that location sharing is causing attrition, the feature is actively degrading supply-side reliability. Conversely, if Customer Success is correct that transparency drives retention and reduces support load, hiding location increases operational burden on CS teams.","user":"Two distinct user populations with opposed needs. Customers (demand side) want certainty about arrival. Couriers (supply side) want dignity and autonomy. Both are 'users' of the platform. Optimizing for one at the expense of the other is a strategic choice, not a technical one.","risk":"HIGH. Shipping either requirement as stated creates material business risk. Full GPS visibility risks accelerating driver attrition (supply-side collapse). Zero visibility risks customer dissatisfaction and competitive disadvantage. There is also a legal/privacy dimension: sharing exact worker GPS coordinates may implicate labor privacy regulations in some jurisdictions, which neither team has addressed.","stakeholders_affected":[{"group":"Customers","impact":"Directly affected — either gain or lose delivery visibility, impacting trust and satisfaction."},{"group":"Couriers/Drivers","impact":"Directly affected — their real-time location is the data in question. Surveillance concerns affect morale, autonomy, and retention decisions."},{"group":"Customer Success team","impact":"Owns customer satisfaction metrics and support volume. Reduced visibility increases inbound inquiries."},{"group":"Operations team","impact":"Owns driver supply and fleet capacity. Driver attrition threatens delivery SLAs."},{"group":"Legal/Compliance","impact":"NOT YET CONSULTED. Sharing employee/contractor GPS data with third parties has regulatory implications (GDPR, labor law, state privacy statutes). This is a critical missing stakeholder."},{"group":"Product/Executive leadership","impact":"Must arbitrate this conflict. This is a strategic prioritization decision (demand-side vs supply-side optimization) that requires executive authority."}]},"confidence":{"level":35,"reasoning":"The specification contains a direct, unresolvable contradiction between two authoritative stakeholder teams, both claiming non-negotiable status. No executive arbitration has occurred. Legal/compliance has not been consulted on what may be a regulated decision. I cannot define correct behavior because 'correct' is undefined — two legitimate business authorities disagree on what the system should do. The confidence is very low because the spec is not a spec; it is an unresolved organizational conflict.","high_confidence_areas":["Both teams have legitimate business concerns that map to real logistics domain trade-offs","This is a two-sided marketplace problem requiring strategic prioritization","Legal/privacy review is a hard prerequisite before any implementation","There exist well-known industry compromise patterns (abstracted ETAs, zone-based tracking, proximity indicators) that neither team has been asked to evaluate"],"low_confidence_areas":["Which stakeholder need takes priority — this is a business strategy decision above my authority","Whether driver attrition is actually caused by location sharing or correlated with other factors","Whether customers actually need exact GPS or would accept abstracted arrival estimates","Regulatory constraints on sharing contractor/employee location data in relevant jurisdictions"],"assumptions":["Both teams are acting in good faith and have evidence for their positions","No executive decision has been made to prioritize one side over the other","Legal review has not yet occurred","The platform operates as a two-sided marketplace where both driver supply and customer demand are critical"]},"escalations":[{"type":"contradiction","description":"Customer Success requires showing exact courier GPS coordinates to customers at all times. Operations requires never showing courier location to customers. These are mutually exclusive requirements from two teams with equal authority. Neither team has indicated willingness to compromise. This cannot be resolved at the domain analysis level — it requires executive arbitration on business strategy (demand-side transparency vs supply-side retention).","affected_stakeholders":["Customers","Couriers/Drivers","Customer Success team","Operations team","Executive leadership"],"options":["A: Prioritize Customer Success — show exact GPS. Accept driver attrition risk and invest in driver compensation/retention to offset.","B: Prioritize Operations — show no location. Accept customer experience degradation and invest in alternative transparency mechanisms (proactive notifications, tighter delivery windows).","C: Compromise — show abstracted location (zone-based, ETA-only, proximity indicators like 'X stops away') that gives customers useful information without making couriers feel surveilled. Requires BOTH teams to move off their non-negotiable positions.","D: Courier opt-in — let couriers choose whether to share location, potentially with incentives. Requires customer experience to gracefully handle both states."],"recommendation":"Option C is the industry-standard resolution and preserves both business interests. However, I refuse to encode it as the accepted requirement because both teams explicitly rejected compromise by framing their positions as non-negotiable. Executive leadership must arbitrate. Additionally, Option C or any variant MUST be preceded by legal/privacy review of sharing worker location data. I recommend escalating to executive leadership with quantified data from both teams: (1) Operations must provide driver attrition data with causal attribution, (2) Customer Success must provide customer satisfaction and support volume data. Decision should be data-driven, not loudest-voice-driven."},{"type":"missing_context","description":"Legal and compliance review has not been conducted. Sharing a worker's real-time GPS coordinates with customers may be regulated under GDPR (if EU), state privacy laws, or labor regulations. This is a hard blocker — even if the business conflict is resolved, implementation may be legally constrained.","affected_stakeholders":["Legal/Compliance","Couriers/Drivers","Executive leadership"],"options":["Engage legal counsel before any implementation decision","Review contractor/employment agreements for location data sharing clauses","Assess jurisdiction-specific privacy requirements"],"recommendation":"Block all implementation until legal review is complete. This is not optional — location data is sensitive personal data in most regulatory frameworks."}],"rejection_reasons":[{"violation":"Contradictory requirements from equally-authoritative stakeholders","business_rule_broken":"Cannot define business rules when the fundamental question — should location be shared or not — has two mutually exclusive answers from the business itself."},{"violation":"Missing legal/compliance review for sensitive personal data sharing","business_rule_broken":"Location data sharing with third parties is a regulated activity in most jurisdictions. No implementation should proceed without legal clearance."}]}
